name: Input
desc: Player steers a vehicle (boat or horse). Sends sideways/forward movement, jump flag, and unmount flag.
notes: Sent every tick while riding a vehicle. Rise 6.0 uses C0CPacketInput in its Disabler module for three different modes: VehicleDisabler (send empty C0C on pre-motion), InputDisabler (send C0C with fix- ed values on strafe), and GhostlyDisabler (send C0C alongside C03 movement). The empty constructor C0CPacketInput() defaults to (0,0,false,false) which freezes the boat.

implementation.overview: C0CPacketInput drives vehicle movement client-side. Four fields: sideways/forward (-1 to 1 floats), jump (boolean — launches boat, charges horse), unmount (boolean — dismount). Rise 6.0 exploits this in three Disabler modes that send spoofed input values alongside movement packets to desync the player's vehicle state.

modules.name: VehicleDisabler (Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Send empty C0CPacketInput every pre-motion to freeze the vehicle while the player moves independently via C03 packets. Creates a desync where the server thinks the player is sitting in a stationary boat but C03 shows movement.
modules.how_it_works: On every PreMotionEvent, sends new C0CPacketInput() — an empty constructor that defaults to (0,0,false,false). This tells the server the vehicle has no input (sideways=0, forward=0, no jump, no unmount). Meanwhile, the module continues sending C03 packets that move the player. The server sees the boat frozen but the player moving — this can bypass vehicle-based anti-cheat checks.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayServer.processInput(C0CPacketInput) — applies input to the ridden entity. PreMotionEvent in Rise fires before movement packets are sent.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Empty C0C packets while the player is moving via C03 creates a clear desync pattern. Watchdog and Grim both detect boats moving without corresponding C0C input (the vehicle position should match C0C forward values). Some anti-cheats also check that the vehicle's actual motion matches the C0C input — a player moving but sending C0C(0,0,false,false) is a flag.

modules.name: InputDisabler (Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Send spoofed C0CPacketInput with maximum forward and no sideways on every strafe event. This is used to override legitimate vehicle input with values that desync anti-cheat tracking.
modules.how_it_works: On StrafeEvent, sends C0CPacketInput(0.98f, 0, true, true) — near-max forward, jump=true, unmount=true. This overwrites whatever legitimate input the player is sending, creating a desync between the vehicle's actual movement and what C0C reports.
modules.vanilla_hook: StrafeEvent — fires during movement calculations. The packet is sent BEFORE the actual movement packet.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Spoofed C0C with jump=true + unmount=true simultaneously is suspicious. No legitimate player sends unmount=true while jumping. Anti-cheats flag this as an invalid input combination.

modules.name: GhostlyDisabler (Rise 6.0)
modules.purpose: Combines C0CPacketInput spoofing with C03 movement manipulation for a comprehensive disabler mode. Sends C0C on every C03 send, and also rounds C03 Y to ground values.
modules.how_it_works: When a C03PacketPlayer is detected, the module first sends C0CPacketInput() (empty — freezes vehicle), then rounds the C03 Y value to the nearest 0.015625 (ground) and forces onGround=true. This combines the vehicle input freeze with movement ground spoofing.
modules.vanilla_hook: PacketSendEvent — fires before every outgoing packet. GhostlyDisabler sends C0C AND modifies C03 in the same event.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: This is a Ghostly (Faithful) HC disabler — combines multiple bypass techniques targeting older anti-cheats. Modern anti-cheats (Grim, Vulcan) detect the C03 ground rounding + forced onGround as invalid movement.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: EntityPlayerSP.onUpdateWalkingPlayer() sends C0C every tick while ridingEntity != null. Vanilla values come from MovementInput. Rise 6.0 hooks via PacketSendEvent/PreMotionEvent.

Server: NetHandlerPlayServer.processInput(C0CPacketInput) applies input to EntityBoat/EntityHorse. Vehicle motion is entirely driven by C0C — not C03.

Rise 6.0 has three distinct C0C disabler modes: VehicleDisabler, InputDisabler, and GhostlyDisabler.
implementation.client_variations:
Rise 6.0: VehicleDisabler (empty C0C on pre-motion), InputDisabler (spoofed values on strafe), GhostlyDisabler (C0C + ground-rounded C03). All three are modes of the Disabler module.
RektSky B5: BoatBoost module spoofs C0C forward=1.0, jump=true.
November 0.2: BoatFly modifies C0C fields directly.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific C0C module.
Sigma 4.11: EventPacket handles C0C but no module specifically targets it.
